items.final_wording: Supports documenting unintentional weight loss, appetite, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, prolonged illness, BMI, percentage weight loss, reduced intake and future intake risk when actually assessed.

source.title: Nutrition support for adults: oral nutrition support, enteral tube feeding and parenteral nutrition — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE nutrition, dietetics, consent, capacity, and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults in hospital and community settings with clinical concern for malnutrition or impaired intake.
source.setting: General practice, community, hospital, nutrition, and multidisciplinary assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting unintentional weight loss, poor appetite, altered taste, impaired swallowing, altered bowel habit, reduced intake, BMI and percentage weight loss when measured. It does not calculate risk or generate nutrition support.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 — screening, clinical concern, weight loss, intake, and swallowing

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting unintentional weight loss, appetite, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, prolonged illness, BMI, percentage weight loss, reduced intake and future intake risk when actually assessed.

items.rationale: The exact screening section supports appetite, intake, weight change, BMI, swallowing, bowel, illness, and functional context without assigning malnutrition.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-recorded malnutrition or risk criteria and consent or best-interest discussion; no diagnosis, calculation, feeding route, or treatment is generated.
